stimulus: Some fish species don't watch over their own young. Instead, adult females release their eggs near other fishes' nests, mixing them with another species' own eggs. Female cuckoo catfish have been observed quickly releasing eggs into the nests of mouthbrooding cichlids while those fish are gathering their own eggs. After the eggs hatch, the noncatfish parents typically tend to the catfish young as if they were their own, even though the catfish look very different from the other fry.
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It introduces a physical feature of female cuckoo catfish that is described later in the text.
It describes the appearance of the cichlid nests mentioned earlier in the text.
It explains how other fish react to the female cuckoo catfish behavior discussed in the text.
It offers a detail about how female cuckoo catfish carry out the behavior discussed in the text.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The first two sentences establish that some fish species lay their eggs in other fishes' nests. The underlined sentence then provides a specific detail about how female cuckoo catfish accomplish this—quickly, while the cichlids are gathering their own eggs.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence describes behavior, not a physical feature. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence doesn't describe the nests' appearance. Choice C is incorrect. The other fishes' reaction is in the final sentence, not the underlined one.
